subject: Public Health
title: Vaccine Hesitancy
description: A quick, practical guide to Vaccine Hesitancy.

hook.text: The WHO named vaccine hesitancy one of the top 10 threats to global health—even before COVID-19.

buildup.text: Hesitancy exists on a spectrum—from mild concern about side effects to outright refusal based on conspiracy theories.

discovery.text: Trust in healthcare providers is the strongest predictor of vaccine acceptance—more than education or income.

twist.text: Dismissing hesitant parents as 'anti-science' backfires—empathetic listening changes more minds than arguing.

climax.text: Tailored, community-based approaches that address specific concerns are more effective than mass campaigns.

punchline.text: Hesitancy isn't ignorance. It's distrust. And trust must be earned.

quiz.question: What is the strongest predictor of vaccine acceptance?
